hypothesis: Integrating a dedicated molecular image detection and segmentation module (e.g., fine-tuned DECIMER) as a preprocessing step within the single-agent pipeline—before LLM-based extraction—will yield statistically significant improvements in SMILES extraction F1 scores across both nanomaterial and small molecule datasets in ChemX, compared to pipelines relying solely on LLM vision captioning, because the near-zero SMILES extraction performance observed across all current methods is primarily attributable to the absence of specialized molecular structure recognition rather than to limitations in the LLM's chemical reasoning capabilities.
